summary: DHH reverses his long-held skepticism of cryptocurrency after watching the Canadian government invoke emergency powers to freeze bank accounts of trucker protest donors. He acknowledges all of crypto's real problems — energy consumption, fraud, lack of decentralization — but argues the Canadian response proved that even stable Western democracies can weaponize financial systems against peaceful protesters. He compares the shock to the Snowden revelations: unbelievable until undeniable. The fundamental freedom to transact, he concludes, is worth protecting even if the current crypto ecosystem is deeply flawed.
key_points: Canada's use of emergency powers to freeze bank accounts of protest donors demonstrated that financial authoritarianism isn't limited to failing states
DHH's prior dismissal of crypto's relevance to Western democracies was fueled by a lack of imagination about how governments could weaponize banking
The fact that this authoritarianism came from Canada rather than Trump's America makes it harder for Westerners to dismiss as an anomaly
The situation parallels the Snowden revelations — seeming hyperbolic until proven undeniable
Crypto's many real flaws don't negate its fundamental value as protection for the freedom to transact
money_quotes: Just because Bitcoin's most virtuous argument was presented – in if not bad-faith then in fig-leaf-faith – by get-rich-quick boosters, doesn't mean it isn't true!
I still can't believe that this is the protest that would prove every Bitcoin crank a prophet.
You might as well have told me that it was really the Care Bears who ran Abu Ghraib.
This is crazy. Absolutely bonkers. Terrifying.

I'm sitting with that same feeling here. Even just a few months ago, I would not have found it credible if you said a three-week peaceful protest in Canada could have lead to martial law, frozen bank accounts, and terrorist-financing laws being used to hunt protest donors.
I don't think we have any idea what kind of radicalizing seeds have been planted by Trudeau with these actions.
In a weird way, I'm glad we all got this warning from Trudeau in Canada and not Trump in America.
You don't need laser eyes or an NFT avatar to appreciate that.
key_insight: The Canadian government's use of emergency financial powers against peaceful protesters proved that the freedom to transact — crypto's core promise — is essential even in Western democracies.
